description_long: This object consists of two large, irregularly shaped stone blocks with rough, jagged edges and a natural, earthy coloration. The surfaces display a mix of tan, brown, and gray hues, with visible stratification and some weathered patches, suggesting sedimentary rock origins. One side features a small rectangular label and a black-and-white scale marker, indicating it may have been cataloged or studied. The stones appear fractured, with a clear split between the two pieces, exposing fresh, angular break lines. Some faces show faint markings or writing, likely for identification purposes. The overall texture is coarse and uneven, with occasional patches of darker mineral deposits.
description: Two fractured, rough-edged stone blocks with tan, brown, and gray hues, featuring visible stratification and weathered surfaces.
description_view_0: Shows the split between the two stone blocks, highlighting their jagged, fractured edges and rough surfaces.
description_view_1: Displays the top and one side of a block, revealing a label, scale marker, and handwritten text on a relatively flat surface.
description_view_2: Emphasizes the rear and side faces, with visible mineral patches and the pronounced split between the stones.
description_view_3: Presents a more unified view of the blocks together, focusing on the broad, flat surfaces and subtle color variations.
attribute_location: urban park, roadside, or landscaped public space
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or used as a natural barrier along paths or perimeters
attribute_pattern: Irregular stratification and layering with some mottled mineral patches; no artificial carvings or regular patterns.
attribute_weathering: Rough and weathered with visible surface erosion, minor chipping, and some fresh fracture lines.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with paved or manicured urban environments but may blend with natural landscaping or other stone features.
